book_claims.mechanism: Small oily fish with dense omega-3 content and low mercury bioaccumulation due to short lifespan and small size
book_claims.text: Anchovies recommended as a high omega-3, omega-6, and vitamin B12 fish; low in mercury

book_claims.mechanism: Source of omega-3 fatty acids, calcium, and vitamin D
book_claims.text: Used on Longevity Diet pizza; source of calcium (66 mg per 1 oz canned, 7% DV) and vitamin D (20 IU per 1 oz, 5% DV)

supplementary_research.finding: Small pelagic fish like anchovies have the lowest mercury contamination among marine species; high EPA/DHA content comparable to sardines
Omega-3 EPA/DHA from oily fish reduces all-cause and cardiovascular mortality; anchovies among best dietary sources per calorie

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Direct marine omega-3s; highest bioavailability among omega-3 sources
Bioavailable form; important for neurological function
From soft bones when eaten whole; good non-dairy calcium source

synergies.note: Both small oily low-mercury fish recommended together for high omega-3 intake in the Longevity Diet
Traditional Mediterranean pairing; fat enhances absorption of fat-soluble vitamins
Lycopene in tomatoes is fat-soluble; anchovy fat enhances absorption
